sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.rendered_content:
This comparison of GOOGL and MSFT uses each company's most recent annual filing and should be read as a comparison of financial profiles rather than as a claim that each company is economically identical.

GOOGL reports $403B of revenue (15.1% YoY) and $132B of net income in 2025-12-31. GOOGL's profile is further defined by net margin of 32.8%, debt-to-equity of 0.12x, free cash flow of $73.3B.

MSFT reports $282B of revenue (14.9% YoY) and $102B of net income in 2025-06-30. MSFT's profile is further defined by net margin of 36.1%, debt-to-equity of 0.13x, free cash flow of $71.6B.

GOOGL is the larger company on reported annual revenue, while MSFT carries the highest net margin, so scale and efficiency do not point to the same leader.

paragraphs.text: This comparison of GOOGL and MSFT uses each company's most recent annual filing and should be read as a comparison of financial profiles rather than as a claim that each company is economically identical.
GOOGL reports $403B of revenue (15.1% YoY) and $132B of net income in 2025-12-31. GOOGL's profile is further defined by net margin of 32.8%, debt-to-equity of 0.12x, free cash flow of $73.3B.
MSFT reports $282B of revenue (14.9% YoY) and $102B of net income in 2025-06-30. MSFT's profile is further defined by net margin of 36.1%, debt-to-equity of 0.13x, free cash flow of $71.6B.
GOOGL is the larger company on reported annual revenue, while MSFT carries the highest net margin, so scale and efficiency do not point to the same leader.

sections.title: Relative Strengths
sections.rendered_content:
For GOOGL, the most favorable feature in the current figures is straightforward: Gross margin of 59.7% leaves the company with a reasonable buffer to absorb operating expenses. ROE of 35.7% points to very strong earnings generation relative to book equity. That picture sits alongside free cash flow of $73.3B and debt-to-equity of 0.12x, which helps show how profitability is balanced against financing risk.

For MSFT, the most favorable feature in the current figures is straightforward: Gross margin of 68.8% leaves the company with a reasonable buffer to absorb operating expenses. ROE of 33.3% points to very strong earnings generation relative to book equity. That picture sits alongside free cash flow of $71.6B and debt-to-equity of 0.13x, which helps show how profitability is balanced against financing risk.

sections.title: Risk Comparison
sections.rendered_content:
The risk discussion is limited to what each annual filing supports directly, and missing peer metrics are left blank rather than forced into a misleading side-by-side comparison.

GOOGL does not present one dominant area of financial pressure in the reporting period used here, although that should not be mistaken for a risk-free profile.

MSFT does not present one dominant area of financial pressure in the reporting period used here, although that should not be mistaken for a risk-free profile.

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.rendered_content:
All peer figures are drawn from annual filings. Missing metrics remain blank, so the note should be read as a comparison of what each filing supports rather than as a fully normalized screen.

GOOGL enters the comparison with clear areas of support in the numbers and no single dominant area of immediate financial stress. Gross margin of 59.7% leaves the company with a reasonable buffer to absorb operating expenses.

MSFT enters the comparison with clear areas of support in the numbers and no single dominant area of immediate financial stress. Gross margin of 68.8% leaves the company with a reasonable buffer to absorb operating expenses.
